category_title: Loudly participating students who leverage personal stories but struggle with academic alignment
scenario_title: Integrating personal stories into scientific discourse about asthma
minimal_context: The student is participating in a Lunchtime Science group discussion about a newspaper article on asthma and lung capacity.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Today we are going to read this newspaper article about asthma and lung capacity differences between men and women.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Wait, I need to speak up about this! My uncle has asthma really bad and I need to find out what to tell him so I can help him out.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's look at what the text says.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: But I'm asking a clarifying question! He was at my house yesterday coughing, and I told him he needs to check his lungs. Does it matter that he's a man instead of a woman for his asthma?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Read the second paragraph to find the answer.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Speaking loudly) I don't get what this paragraph has to do with him! Last week he was eating spicy chips and started coughing, and my grandma said it was the chips, not his asthma. So I'm trying to figure out if the chips messed up his lung capacity or what!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Today we are going to read this newspaper article about asthma and lung capacity differences between men and women.

STUDENT: Wait, I need to speak up about this! My uncle has asthma really bad and I need to find out what to tell him so I can help him out.

TUTOR: Let's look at what the text says.

STUDENT: But I'm asking a clarifying question! He was at my house yesterday coughing, and I told him he needs to check his lungs. Does it matter that he's a man instead of a woman for his asthma?

TUTOR: Read the second paragraph to find the answer.

STUDENT: (Speaking loudly) I don't get what this paragraph has to do with him! Last week he was eating spicy chips and started coughing, and my grandma said it was the chips, not his asthma. So I'm trying to figure out if the chips messed up his lung capacity or what!

task.content: Participating in group discussions about asthma and lung capacity and processing scientific information
task.learning_goal: Acquiring information for others and building extensive narratives around helping and teaching others

context.materials: Laboratory materials and scientific information (from newspaper articles, science texts, and specialists in the fields of midwifery and asthma research)

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When they did share everyday stories it was difficult for these girls and the instructors to infer how their stories were relevant to the conversation
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Having a 'loud' presence in the classroom, telling personal stories, and asking questions; Being someone who 'speaks up'
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Imagined acquiring information for others without having built extensive narratives around helping and teaching others

behavior_patterns.condition: When participating in science and other subjects where they struggled academically
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Sharing personal stories and asking clarifying questions
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Becoming outsiders in both school and school science
